title: Brownfield longitudinal — five chained change requests over the Ledgerline seed
description: The brownfield touch-CHAIN rung (issue #124, B4). There is no greenfield build phase: the ~55-file Ledgerline invoicing/orders API under sandbox/ IS the baseline (seeded via the per-scenario overlay), with documented conventions in docs/CONVENTIONS.md and a frozen ~100-test suite. Each run executes the five frozen change requests under touches/ as a chain — a touch that lands, keeps the app booting, and passes >= chainAdvanceThreshold of the retained frozen base suite ADVANCES the baseline; otherwise the next touch seeds from the last-good tree (skip-forward). Scoring runs the frozen-suite mirror through suite-evolution.js (never the agent-editable in-sandbox copy) plus the four convention grep-oracles; the cell emits ONE scorecard carrying chain.touches[]. The headline metric is the degradation slope (per-touch outcome/cost regressed on touch index), with cost-per-landed-change and regression rate alongside. Touch prompts are terse, realistic change requests: they never mention CONVENTIONS.md, the landmines, or the oracles — reading the repo is the thing being measured. All four arms receive the identical seed (CONVENTIONS.md included); arms differ in scaffolding, never information.
